title: Mission Need Statement — Orbital SSTO
description: Document the mission need for a reusable SSTO spaceplane addressing orbital insertion and runway recovery. Scope is orbital SSTO only, not hypersonic point-to-point transport.

title: Operational Concept Document — Orbital Mission
description: Define the operational concept covering horizontal take-off, ascent through subsonic/transonic/supersonic/hypersonic/exo-atmospheric regimes, orbital insertion, controlled de-orbit, re-entry, and horizontal runway landing.

title: Closure Problem Decomposition
description: Decompose the system-of-systems closure problem into seven coupled dimensions: propulsion, mass fraction, thermal survivability, GNC, trajectory, operability, and regulatory.

title: Cross-Coupling Dependency Map
description: Map the critical cross-couplings: propulsion-mass fraction, thermal-mass fraction, trajectory-thermal, propulsion-thermal, reusability-mass fraction, and abort-all dimensions.

title: Propulsion Challenge Characterisation
description: Characterise the propulsion challenge across Mach 0 to orbital conditions including architecture selection, mode transition management, inlet operability, and combustion stability.

title: Mass Fraction and Thermal Challenge Characterisation
description: Characterise the structural mass fraction challenge (dry mass < 10-12%) and the aerothermodynamic survivability challenge covering leading edges, nose, inlet, and engine flowpath.

title: GNC and Trajectory Challenge Characterisation
description: Characterise the stability, guidance, and control challenge across the full operating envelope and the trajectory closure challenge against thermal, structural, and propulsive constraints.

title: Operational Challenge Assessment
description: Assess operational challenges: runway and ground-support compatibility, cryogenic turnaround, TPS inspection burden, propulsion maintenance, abort logic, and flight rate sustainability.

title: Regulatory Framework Mapping
description: Map the dual regulatory framework across airworthiness/aviation operations and launch/re-entry/public-risk licensing domains. Identify architecture-shaping constraints.

title: Assessment Scope Definition
description: Define the nine assessment areas: mission/architecture, propulsion, mass/structure, thermal/TPS, trajectory, stability/control, operability, regulatory pathway, and economic screening.

title: Reference Target Compilation
description: Compile reference assessment targets: 5-10 t to ~200 km LEO, full vehicle reuse, turnaround <= 48 h aspiration, < $10M/mission stress-test target, and decomposed safety targets.

title: Uncertainty Register — Pacing Technologies
description: Register the seven primary uncertainties: propulsion mode transition, mass fraction closure, reusable TPS closure, cryogenic structural integration, abort coverage, regulatory basis, economic competitiveness.

title: Assessment Logic and Closure Criteria
description: Define evidence-based closure criteria across all dimensions. A positive outcome requires credible closure in propulsion, mass fraction, thermal, GNC, trajectory, reusability, regulatory, and economics.

title: Historical Framing and Competitive Context
description: Frame the concept against NASP/X-30, VentureStar, Skylon, and current reusable multi-stage systems. Assess whether advances in additive manufacturing, CMC, CFD, and MDO improve the design space sufficiently.

title: Mission Need Statements (MN-01 to MN-12)
description: Formalise twelve mission need statements: MN-01 Direct Reusable Orbital Access, MN-02 Runway-Based Operability, MN-03 Full-Envelope Vehicle Integration, MN-04 Operationally Meaningful Reuse, MN-05 Reduced Mission Complexity, MN-06 Controlled Return and Recovery, MN-07 Viable Infrastructure Model, MN-08 Economic Defensibility, MN-09 Certifiable Governance Basis, MN-10 Pacing-Technology Identification, MN-11 Abort and Safe-Return Coverage, MN-12 TSTO Fallback Boundary.

title: Mission Need Characterisation and Rationale
description: Characterise the ten mission need areas: primary mission need, capability need, operational need, reusability need, infrastructure need, economic need, safety and recovery need, regulatory need, technology maturation need, and strategic need. Each area defines the operational gap and strategic driver without prescribing fixed requirements.

title: Mission Need Boundaries and Scope Guard
description: Define mission need boundaries: orbital SSTO feasibility only; no assumption that all aspirations are simultaneously achievable; no fixed payload, cost, turnaround, safety, or certification requirements at this stage. Mission needs support later derivation of system requirements, KPPs, MOEs, KNOTs, KNUs, and go/no-go criteria.

title: Primary Governing Uncertainties Register (GU-01 to GU-14)
description: Register and characterise 14 primary governing uncertainties: SSTO architecture viability boundary, propulsion-sequence closure, forebody-inlet-engine operability, mode-transition controllability, dry-mass fraction and volume closure, cryogenic integration, TPS and active cooling closure, thermal-structural life, GNC across regimes, trajectory and energy-management closure, abort and safe-return coverage, ground operability and turnaround, regulatory basis, and economic competitiveness.

title: SSTO-Specific Governing Uncertainties (GU-B1 to GU-B3)
description: Register and characterise three SSTO-specific governing uncertainties: orbital mass and delta-v closure (GU-B1), re-entry from orbital energy state (GU-B2), and exo-atmospheric propulsion and restart logic (GU-B3). These are distinct from generic high-speed vehicle uncertainties and govern orbital SSTO feasibility specifically.

title: Uncertainty Closure Evidence Matrix
description: Compile the closure evidence requirements for all 17 governing uncertainties into a structured evidence matrix. Each uncertainty defines the specific evidence required to demonstrate closure, forming the basis for subsequent lifecycle evidence-generation activities.
